notes: institution_control rules are DEFERRED until CONTROL field is re-ingested into Bronze parquet (see EDA Critical Finding #2). CIP code format uses corrected pattern ^\d{2}\.\d{2}$ per EDA Critical Finding #1.

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: unitid x cipcode x credential_level
rules.sql: SELECT unitid, cipcode, credential_level, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.college_scorecard GROUP BY unitid, cipcode, credential_level HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per unitid x cipcode x credential_level. Any duplicate combination indicates a transformer or promote failure. This is the structural foundation of the table.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'Grain uniqueness (unitid x cipcode x credlev) | 0 duplicates | PASS'. 69,947 rows checked, 0 violations. Threshold: 0 duplicates (100% unique).

rules.name: CIP code format after normalization
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE cipcode IS NULL OR cipcode !~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2,4}$'
rules.description: After CIP code normalization (inserting dot at position 2), all cipcode values must match the pattern XX.XX (or XX.XXXX if zero-padded). The EDA found all 69,947 source codes are 4-digit, producing XX.XX format. The regex allows 2-4 digits after the dot to accommodate both formats.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'All 69,947 codes will produce valid XX.XX format strings. Zero codes will fail the transformation itself.' Pattern corrected from physical model CHECK per EDA Critical Finding #1.

rules.name: CIP family is valid 2-digit code
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE cip_family IS NULL OR cip_family !~ '^\d{2}$'
rules.description: The cip_family field is derived as the first 2 characters of the normalized cipcode. It must be a 2-digit string. All 45 observed families are valid CIP 2020 codes.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'All 45 families are valid CIP 2020 two-digit codes. No unknown or invalid family codes exist.' 69,947 rows, 45 distinct families, 0 invalid.

rules.name: CIP family matches cipcode prefix
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE cip_family != SUBSTRING(cipcode, 1, 2)
rules.description: The cip_family field must equal the first 2 characters of cipcode. This validates the derivation logic is correct.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA derivation rule: 'cip_family = cipcode[:2]'. All 69,947 rows should match. 0 expected violations.

rules.name: unitid not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE unitid IS NULL
rules.description: unitid is a NOT NULL natural key field. Must be present in every row.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA Required Field Null Check: 'unitid | Null Count: 0'. 69,947 rows, 0% null.

rules.name: institution_name not null or empty
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE institution_name IS NULL OR TRIM(institution_name) = ''
rules.description: institution_name is a NOT NULL field. Must be non-empty in every row.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA Required Field Null Check: 'institution_name | Null Count: 0 | Empty Count: 0'. 69,947 rows.

rules.name: cipcode not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE cipcode IS NULL
rules.description: cipcode is a NOT NULL natural key field. Must be present in every row.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA Required Field Null Check: 'cipcode | Null Count: 0'. 69,947 rows, 0% null.

rules.name: program_name not null or empty
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE program_name IS NULL OR TRIM(program_name) = ''
rules.description: program_name is a NOT NULL field. Must be non-empty in every row.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA Required Field Null Check: 'program_name | Null Count: 0 | Empty Count: 0'. 69,947 rows.

rules.name: credential_level not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE credential_level IS NULL
rules.description: credential_level is a NOT NULL natural key field. Must be present in every row.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA Required Field Null Check: 'credential_level | Null Count: 0'. 69,947 rows, 0% null.

rules.name: credential_description not null or empty
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE credential_description IS NULL OR TRIM(credential_description) = ''
rules.description: credential_description is a NOT NULL field. Must be non-empty in every row.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA Required Field Null Check: 'credential_description | Null Count: 0 | Empty Count: 0'. 69,947 rows.

rules.name: cip_family not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE cip_family IS NULL
rules.description: cip_family is a NOT NULL derived field. Since cipcode is 0% null and cip_family is derived from cipcode, it must always be populated.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'cip_family | Max Null Rate: 0% | Derived from cipcode, which is 0% null.' 69,947 rows.

rules.name: cip_family_name not null or empty
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE cip_family_name IS NULL OR TRIM(cip_family_name) = ''
rules.description: cip_family_name is a NOT NULL derived field. All 45 observed CIP families must resolve in the lookup table.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'cip_family_name | Max Null Rate: 0% | Derived via lookup, all 45 families must resolve.' Families 32,33,34,35,36,53 are unusual at bachelor's level (52 rows) but must be included.

rules.name: small_cohort_flag not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE small_cohort_flag IS NULL
rules.description: small_cohort_flag is a NOT NULL derived boolean. Must be True or False for every row.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'small_cohort_flag | Max Null Rate: 0% | Derived, always generated.' Expected True=75.52%, False=24.48%.

rules.name: source_load_date not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE source_load_date IS NULL
rules.description: source_load_date is a NOT NULL pipeline metadata field.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA Required Field Null Check: 'source_load_date | Null Count: 0'. 69,947 rows, 0% null.

rules.name: ingested_at not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE ingested_at IS NULL
rules.description: ingested_at is a NOT NULL pipeline metadata timestamp, generated at transformation time.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'ingested_at | Max Null Rate: 0% | Generated at transformation time.' Must always be populated.

rules.name: earnings_1yr_median null rate within bounds
rules.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN earnings_1yr_median IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS null_pct FROM base.college_scorecard
rules.description: The earnings_1yr_median field is nullable due to privacy suppression. The null rate should not exceed 70%. A higher rate would suggest a source data change.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'earnings_1yr_median | Max Null Rate: 70% | Source: 64.0% null.' Current null rate is 64.0% (44,751 of 69,947). Threshold set at 70% with headroom per EDA recommendation.

rules.name: earnings_2yr_median null rate within bounds
rules.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN earnings_2yr_median IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS null_pct FROM base.college_scorecard
rules.description: The earnings_2yr_median field is nullable due to privacy suppression. The null rate should not exceed 65%. A higher rate would suggest a source data change.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'earnings_2yr_median | Max Null Rate: 65% | Source: 60.4% null.' Current null rate is 60.4% (42,266 of 69,947). Threshold set at 65% with headroom per EDA recommendation.

rules.name: debt_median null rate within bounds
rules.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN debt_median IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS null_pct FROM base.college_scorecard
rules.description: The debt_median field is nullable due to privacy suppression. The null rate should not exceed 68%. A higher rate would suggest a source data change.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'debt_median | Max Null Rate: 68% | Source: 63.1% null.' Current null rate is 63.1% (44,138 of 69,947). Threshold set at 68% with headroom per EDA recommendation.

rules.name: completions_count_1 null rate within bounds
rules.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN completions_count_1 IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS null_pct FROM base.college_scorecard
rules.description: The completions_count_1 field has a low null rate. An increase beyond 12% would suggest a source change.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'completions_count_1 | Max Null Rate: 12% | Source: 8.7% null.' Current: 6,098 of 69,947 rows null (8.72%). Threshold set at 12% with headroom.

rules.name: completions_count_2 null rate within bounds
rules.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN completions_count_2 IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS null_pct FROM base.college_scorecard
rules.description: The completions_count_2 field has a low null rate. An increase beyond 12% would suggest a source change.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'completions_count_2 | Max Null Rate: 12% | Source: 8.3% null.' Threshold set at 12% with headroom.

rules.name: Row count within expected range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) >= 60000 AND COUNT(*) <= 80000 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.college_scorecard
rules.description: The Silver base table should contain 60,000-80,000 rows. The current source has 69,947 rows. A count outside this range indicates a transformation filter error or source data change.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'Record Count: 69,947.' Bronze EDA established range 60,000-80,000. Silver should match raw count (1:1 transformation, no row-level filtering).

rules.name: Earnings 1yr range validation
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE earnings_1yr_median IS NOT NULL AND (earnings_1yr_median < 1000 OR earnings_1yr_median > 250000)
rules.description: When present, earnings_1yr_median must be between $1,000 and $250,000. This matches the physical model CHECK constraint. The EDA observed range is $4,880-$161,723 with 0 violations.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'earnings_1yr_median >= 1000 AND <= 250000 | Violations: 0 | PASS'. Source range: $4,880-$161,723. 0 of 25,196 non-null values outside bounds.

rules.name: Earnings 2yr range validation
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE earnings_2yr_median IS NOT NULL AND (earnings_2yr_median < 1000 OR earnings_2yr_median > 250000)
rules.description: When present, earnings_2yr_median must be between $1,000 and $250,000. This matches the physical model CHECK constraint. The EDA observed range is $5,938-$160,116 with 0 violations.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'earnings_2yr_median >= 1000 AND <= 250000 | Violations: 0 | PASS'. Source range: $5,938-$160,116. 0 of 27,681 non-null values outside bounds.

rules.name: Debt range validation
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE debt_median IS NOT NULL AND (debt_median < 1000 OR debt_median > 100000)
rules.description: When present, debt_median must be between $1,000 and $100,000. This matches the physical model CHECK constraint. The EDA observed range is $2,750-$57,500 with 0 violations.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'debt_median >= 1000 AND <= 100000 | Violations: 0 | PASS'. Source range: $2,750-$57,500. 0 of 25,809 non-null values outside bounds.

rules.name: credential_level must equal 3
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE credential_level != 3
rules.description: This MVP dataset is filtered to bachelor's degrees only (credential_level=3). Any other value indicates a filter failure in the pipeline.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'credential_level BETWEEN 1 AND 8 | Violations: 0 | PASS (all = 3)'. Domain context: 'CREDLEV must equal 3. This dataset is filtered to bachelor's degrees only. Any non-3 value indicates an ingestor filter failure.'

rules.name: small_cohort_flag consistency with completions_count_1
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE small_cohort_flag != (completions_count_1 IS NULL OR completions_count_1 < 30)
rules.description: The small_cohort_flag must exactly match the derivation rule: True when completions_count_1 IS NULL OR completions_count_1 < 30, False otherwise. This validates the derived field logic.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'small_cohort_flag accuracy: flag = (completions_count_1 IS NULL OR completions_count_1 < 30) | Expected: 100% match.' True=52,826 (75.52%), False=17,121 (24.48%).

rules.name: institution_control valid values
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE institution_control NOT IN ('Public', 'Private nonprofit', 'Private for-profit')
rules.description: institution_control must be one of the three allowed values per the physical model CHECK constraint. DEFERRED: CONTROL field is not yet in Bronze parquet (EDA Critical Finding #2). This rule is written for when the field is populated.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'CONTROL column is NOT in the Bronze parquet.' Physical model CHECK: IN ('Public', 'Private nonprofit', 'Private for-profit'). Rule written proactively; will validate after re-ingestion.

rules.name: Record count consistency with raw zone
rules.sql: SELECT ABS(silver.cnt - raw.cnt) * 100.0 / raw.cnt FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.college_scorecard) silver, (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.college_scorecard) raw
rules.description: The Silver base table row count should be within 5% of the raw zone count. The Silver transformation is a 1:1 row mapping (no filtering, no aggregation), so counts should match exactly. The 5% threshold allows for minor pipeline variations.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'Record Count: 69,947' matches Bronze source. The transformation does not add or remove rows. A >5% deviation indicates a pipeline error.

rules.name: Distinct institution count within range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT unitid) >= 2200 AND COUNT(DISTINCT unitid) <= 3000 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.college_scorecard
rules.description: The Silver table should contain 2,200-3,000 distinct institutions. The current source has 2,559 distinct UNITIDs. A count outside this range indicates a data loss or unexpected source expansion.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'Distinct institutions: 2,559.' Bronze EDA established range 2,200-3,000. Domain context: There are approximately 2,500+ bachelor's-granting institutions in the U.S.

rules.name: Completions counts non-negative when present
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE (completions_count_1 IS NOT NULL AND completions_count_1 < 0) OR (completions_count_2 IS NOT NULL AND completions_count_2 < 0)
rules.description: Completions counts are non-negative integers representing IPEDS completion volumes. Negative values are impossible in this domain.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'completions_count_1 >= 0 | Violations: 0 | PASS' and 'completions_count_2 >= 0 | Violations: 0 | PASS'. Domain context: 'Completions counts must be non-negative when present.'

rules.name: record_id not null and unique
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.college_scorecard GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'NULL_CHECK' AS record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE record_id IS NULL HAVING cnt > 0
rules.description: record_id is the deterministic surrogate primary key. It must be non-null and unique across all rows. Duplicates would indicate a grain hash collision or derivation bug.
rules.evidence: Physical model: 'record_id | PRIMARY KEY | NOT NULL'. Derived via compute_grain_id() with prefix 'cs'. Deterministic SHA-256 hash should produce 0 collisions for 69,947 rows.

rules.name: Distinct CIP families within expected range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT cip_family) >= 40 AND COUNT(DISTINCT cip_family) <= 50 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.college_scorecard
rules.description: The Silver table should contain 40-50 distinct CIP families. The current source has exactly 45 distinct families. This is a stable count tied to the CIP 2020 taxonomy.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'Distinct CIP families: 40-50 | Source: 45. Stable (CIP taxonomy).' All 45 are valid CIP 2020 codes.

rules.name: Distinct CIP codes within expected range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT cipcode) >= 350 AND COUNT(DISTINCT cipcode) <= 450 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.college_scorecard
rules.description: The Silver table should contain 350-450 distinct CIP codes. The current source has 390 distinct codes.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'Distinct CIP codes: 350-450 | Source: 390. Carried from Bronze EDA.'

rules.name: unitid range validation
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.college_scorecard WHERE unitid < 100000 OR unitid > 999999
rules.description: IPEDS UNITIDs are 6-digit identifiers in the range 100,000-999,999. Values outside this range indicate data corruption or a non-IPEDS identifier.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'unitid | Min: 100,654 | Max: 497,268'. Domain context: 'UNITID must be a 6-digit positive integer. IPEDS IDs are assigned in the 6-digit range (100,000-999,999).'

rules.name: small_cohort_flag True rate within expected range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN small_cohort_flag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) >= 70.0 AND ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN small_cohort_flag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) <= 80.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.college_scorecard
rules.description: The small_cohort_flag True rate should be between 70-80%. A rate outside this range would suggest a change in source data completions distribution or a derivation threshold change.
rules.evidence: Silver EDA: 'small_cohort_flag = True: 52,826 rows (75.52%)'. EDA recommendation: 'Set expected True rate at 70-80%. Alert if outside this range.'
